_note:
Machine-readable projection of the terminology contract. The Markdown files in this directory (TERMINOLOGY*.md) hold the doctrine — voice rules, anti-pattern descriptions, principles. This JSON holds every concrete word, every translation pair, every closed list the doctrine refers to.

Flat structure: every term is one entry in `terms[]` with shape { key, category, en, de?, fr?, de_ch?, _note? }. Locale fields are optional; the loader applies the fallback chain de_ch → de → en and fr → en.

Non-term data (formal-pronoun lists, noun-gender map, style conventions, anti-pattern drift examples, error-message patterns, toast conventions, deCH overrides) stays as separate top-level sections because their shapes don't match the flat term entry.

Consumed by services/docs/tests/. See `_categories` for the meaning of each term's `category` field.
_fallbackRules: Each term entry has a required `en` field. Optional locale fields override: `de_ch` falls back to `de`, which falls back to `en`; `fr` falls back to `en`. A term with only `en` set (no `de`, `fr`, `de_ch`) is used unchanged in every locale — typical of brands, acronyms, code identifiers, and established loanwords (`Workflow`, `Dashboard`, `Webhook`).

_categories.brand: Brand and product names (Tale, Convex, OpenRouter, GitHub). Always English everywhere.
_categories.acronym: Initialisms (AI, LLM, API, MCP). Some have native expansions (AI → KI/IA); the abbreviation itself usually stays English.
_categories.codeIdentifier: Environment variables, CLI flags, file paths, API paths, i18n keys, JSON keys. International — never translate. Documented by category; individual identifiers are not enumerated here.
_categories.role: The six platform roles (Owner/Admin/Developer/Editor/Member/Disabled). DE/FR UIs ship translated forms.
_categories.feature: Product features and UI surfaces (Knowledge base, Chat with AI, Canvas, Composer).
_categories.knowledgeEntity: Knowledge-base entity types managed by the user (Customer, Vendor, Product, Document, Folder).
_categories.technicalVocab: Universal tech terms (Token, Prompt, Webhook, Pipeline). Bucket 2 in most cases — kept English in DE/FR.
_categories.actionVerb: Button labels and menu actions (Save, Delete, Edit, Log in).
_categories.deploymentVocab: Install/operate/configure terms (Self-hosted, On-premises, Open source, Zero-downtime).
_categories.loanword: Bucket 2 — established English loanword used unchanged in DE/FR. `de`/`fr` either omitted or equal to `en`.
_categories.gitDomain: Bucket 2a — Git/source-control vocabulary (Pull Request, Merge, Rebase). Stays English in DE/FR. Half-translated forms fail review.
_categories.translateBucket: Bucket 3 — must translate. `de` and `fr` differ from `en`; EN form is rejected in DE/FR prose by `loanword.test.ts`.
_categories.abbreviation: Shorthand forms used in tooltips and tight UI (e.g., z. B., p. ex.). `_note` carries the expansion.

Plural: `Branches`. Avoid `Zweig` (literal translation; reads as undigested German).

Verb: `forken`.

The default branch name. Don't translate.
for example / zum Beispiel / par exemple. DE uses non-breaking space between period and letter (Duden).
that is / das heißt / c'est-à-dire.

formalPronouns._note: Words that signal the wrong politeness form. Consumed by services/docs/tests/terminology.test.ts (hard-fail). Sentence-initial `Sie` in DE is heuristically allowed because it can be the capitalised third-person plural `sie`.

nounGenders._note: Closed list of high-frequency Tale nouns with their grammatical gender. Consumed by services/docs/tests/grammar-de.test.ts (warn-only) to flag article/adjective gender disagreement. Codes: m = masculine, f = feminine, n = neuter. Add a noun here only after verifying with Duden; ambiguous-gender words (e.g. Code Review) are deliberately omitted.

styleConventions._note: Per-locale machine-readable formatting rules. Available for future linters and formatters; not currently consumed by tests.

de.compoundHyphenation: for English loanwords and abbreviations only

fr.apostrophe: ' (typographic in docs prose, ASCII in JSON)

marketingSofteners._note: Words and phrases that mark marketing softening. Strike on sight. The `strike` form is the bad word/phrase; the `fix` field names the replacement (or `delete` when the imperative carries the meaning).

en.fix: (delete; let the demonstration carry the claim)
(delete; describe the missing-step that makes it seamless)
(delete; let the screenshot or walkthrough carry the claim)

de.fix: (delete; the demonstration carries it)
(delete)
(delete)
(delete or describe what makes it convenient)
(delete)
(delete or replace with a concrete capability)
(delete; the screenshot shows it)
(delete; describe the missing-step that makes it seamless)
(delete; imperative does the work)
(replace with Lies, Öffne, Sieh dir … an)
(delete or replace with the concrete action)
(delete; let the reader see)

fr.strike: N'hésite pas à / N'hésitez pas à
il te suffit de / il vous suffit de
s'il te plaît / s'il vous plaît

antiPatternExamples._note: Concrete drift → target pairs for each named anti-pattern. The anti-pattern entries in `antiPatterns` name and describe the failure; this section holds the example pairs reviewers and writers can scan. Format: { drift, target, why? }.

firstPersonWe.target: Click Save. The provider is reachable from agents on the next request.

royalItIs.drift: It is important to note that…
It is recommended to run tale deploy.

nakedCommand.target: Click Save. The provider is reachable from agents on the next request.

hypeSentence.drift: Tale's powerful automation engine lets you simply discover the seamless way to build workflows.
hypeSentence.target: Tale's automation engine runs multi-step workflows on schedules, events, and webhooks.

passivePresent.why: Passive present hides the agent (the system) and adds three characters.

erfolgreichSuffix.why: The toast is the success signal; erfolgreich is redundant.

damitOpener.drift: Damit werden Agents, Workflows und Anbieter entfernt.
Damit kannst du die Konversation fortsetzen.
damitOpener.target: So setzt du die Konversation fort. (or: Setzt du …)

compoundStack.target: Zugriff auf die Mitglieder der Organisation verwalten

calquedIdioms.target: Sofort einsatzbereit or describe what's pre-configured

englishMidSentence.drift: Schicke deinen Request an die API.
englishMidSentence.target: Schicke deine Anfrage an die API.

englishMidSentence.drift: Dieser Agent kümmert sich um Billing-Fragen.
englishMidSentence.target: Dieser Agent kümmert sich um Abrechnungsfragen.

englishMidSentence.drift: Du kannst die Workflow als Self-hosted laufen lassen.
englishMidSentence.target: Du kannst den Workflow selbst gehostet laufen lassen.

sieSlips.target: Vier Entscheidungen — dieselben Knöpfe halten überall.

sieSlips.drift: Wenden Sie sich an den Support.

lazyCapitalisation.target: selbst gehostet (in prose) or Self-Hosted (as proper noun)

genderSlip.why: Warnung is feminine — eine einmalige (fem) not einen einmaligen (masc acc).
Anfrage is feminine — der (dat fem) not dem (dat masc/neut).

marketingSoftening.drift: Découvre comment configurer un agent en toute simplicité.
N'hésite pas à téléverser tes documents.
Il te suffit de cliquer sur **Sauvegarder** pour valider.
Profite d'une intégration clé en main avec ton fournisseur IA.
marketingSoftening.target: Ajoute un Fournisseur IA et choisis le modèle.

marketingSoftening.drift: Bénéficie de la puissance de Tale.

stackedNominalPhrases.drift: Une solution clé en main pour la gestion documentaire intégrée multilingue.
stackedNominalPhrases.target: Une solution qui gère les documents dans plusieurs langues.

stackedNominalPhrases.drift: Le système de notification d'événements de workflow par webhook personnalisable.
stackedNominalPhrases.target: Des notifications de workflow par webhook, configurables.

stackedNominalPhrases.drift: L'interface de configuration des règles de routage des fournisseurs.
stackedNominalPhrases.target: L'interface qui configure le routage entre fournisseurs.

calquedIdioms.target: Nos certifications or Notre posture sécurité

englishMidSentence.drift: Un workflow d'exemple pour Sales Research.
englishMidSentence.target: Un workflow d'exemple pour la recherche commerciale.

englishMidSentence.drift: Cet agent gère les questions Billing.
englishMidSentence.target: Cet agent gère les questions de facturation.

englishMidSentence.drift: Tu peux faire tourner le workflow en Self-hosted.
englishMidSentence.target: Tu peux faire tourner le workflow en auto-hébergé.

lazyCapitalisation.target: Chat avec l'IA (l'IA lowercase with typographic apostrophe)
webhook d'agent or Webhook d'agent (capitalise only first letter)

halfTranslatedCompound.target: Branch or Fork — never both halves

toastConventions._note: State-change confirmation toasts. Past tense / past participle, no period, the noun comes first. Strike the success adverb — the toast IS the success signal.

errorMessagePatterns.fr: Saisis une adresse de courriel valide.
Adresse de courriel ou mot de passe incorrect.

errorMessagePatterns.en: Couldn't reach the server. Try again.
errorMessagePatterns.de: Der Server ist nicht erreichbar. Versuch es erneut.

errorMessagePatterns.en: You don't have permission to do that.
errorMessagePatterns.de: Du hast nicht die nötige Berechtigung.
errorMessagePatterns.fr: Tu n'as pas la permission de faire ça.

errorMessagePatterns.en: Use letters, numbers, and dashes only.
You've reached the limit for this plan.
errorMessagePatterns.de: Du hast das Limit dieses Plans erreicht.
errorMessagePatterns.fr: Tu as atteint la limite de ce plan.

antiPatterns._note: Named patterns the test suite enforces or that reviewers flag on sight. Documented here for cross-reference; the test logic lives in services/docs/tests/.

shared.name: Stub Closing
shared.fix: Rename closing section; replace bare link list with recap paragraph + contextualised link.

shared.name: Naked List
shared.fix: Add prose paragraph before list; the heading owns a paragraph, not a list.

shared.name: Marketing Drift
shared.fix: Strike the marketing softener (simply, easy, powerful, seamless, discover, …).

shared.name: Half-Translated Compound
shared.test: review (terminology.test.ts where the half is a known UI-bucket term)
shared.fix: Translate the whole compound or keep the whole English form. Never one of each.

de.name: Passive present (Wird X…)
de.fix: Use active verb: Speichert… not Wird gespeichert…

de.name: Sentence-final erfolgreich
de.fix: Strike erfolgreich from confirmation toasts.

de.name: Damit-Opener
de.fix: Verb-first construction: Entfernt… not Damit werden…

de.name: Compound Stack
de.fix: Split compounds longer than three roots.

de.name: Sie-Slip
de.fix: Rewrite to du-form.

de.name: English noun in prose
de.fix: Use the native term from the relevant `terms` entry.

de.name: Gender Slip
de.fix: Make the article/adjective agree with the noun's gender from `nounGenders.de`.

de.name: ß/ss mix in base German
de.fix: Base German (docs/de/) uses ß. Swiss German (docs/de-CH/) uses ss exclusively. Mixing inside one locale is a bug.

fr.name: Marketing softening
fr.fix: Strike Découvre, N'hésite pas à, tout simplement, il te suffit de, clé en main, profite/bénéficie.

fr.name: Stacked nominal phrase
fr.fix: Replace de-de-de chains with relative clause.

fr.name: English noun in prose
fr.fix: Use the native term from the relevant `terms` entry.

fr.name: Wrong apostrophe in fr.json
fr.fix: ASCII ' in JSON; typographic ' only in docs prose.

fr.name: Missing NBSP before colon
fr.fix: Insert U+00A0 before :;!?% in prose; preserve in JSON.

deCH._note: Swiss German (de-CH) overrides on top of the German base. Sparse — only what differs. Mechanics: ß → ss substitution everywhere; period as decimal separator; apostrophe as thousands separator; CHF currency.

legalAuthorities.name: LPD / FADP / DSG
legalAuthorities.expansion: Loi sur la protection des données / Federal Act on Data Protection / Bundesgesetz über den Datenschutz

doNotOverride: Product feature names (Workflow, Dashboard, Canvas, Prompt-Bibliothek)
Role names (Inhaber, Admin, Entwickler, Redakteur, Mitglied, Deaktiviert)
Code, command output, environment variable names, CLI flags
API endpoints, JSON keys, error codes
External brand names (Tale, Convex, OpenRouter, GitHub)
Translate-bucket terms (Kopfzeile, Anfrage, Anbieter, E-Mail, Hilfe-Center, Abrechnung, Vertriebs-Recherche, Entwurf, Anhang, selbst gehostet)
Voice rules — du, active verbs, no marketing softening, no sentence-final erfolgreich

_versionNote: v4.0.0 — flat `terms[]` structure. Every concrete word lives as one entry with shape { key, category, en, de?, fr?, de_ch?, _note? } and the fallback chain de_ch → de → en, fr → en. Replaces v3's nested productVocabulary / enToLocale / establishedLoanwords / gitDomainLoanwords / alwaysEnglish / roleNames / translateBucket sections. Non-term data (formalPronouns, nounGenders, styleConventions, marketingSofteners, antiPatternExamples, toastConventions, errorMessagePatterns, antiPatterns, deCH) stays as separate sections because their shapes don't match the term entry.
